operation: First, create a torus: initialize a top workplane, move the sketch center to (2, 0), draw a 1-unit radius circle, then revolve the sketch 360 degrees around an axis running from point (-2, 1, 0) to (-2, 0, 0). Next, create a cylindrical cutting body: initialize a front workplane, apply a coordinate offset of (0, 3, -5), draw a 1-unit radius circle, extrude the sketch 10 units along the workplane normal. Perform a boolean cut to remove the cylinder's volume from the torus. Finally, select the face at the maximum Y coordinate of the resulting solid and apply a 0.3-unit radius fillet to its edges.
geometry: The model is a modified smooth circular ring (torus) solid with a straight cylindrical through-cut intersecting its body to create an opening in the ring structure. The edge of the cut opening facing the positive Y direction is rounded with a smooth curved fillet to eliminate sharp edges at that transition.
function: This is a rounded pass-through component designed for routing cables, wires, or flexible tubing, with smooth edges to prevent abrasion of the routed material, suitable for installation in enclosures or panels requiring a low-profile wear-resistant opening for flexible lines.
